FR-01.title: Product Catalog Management
requirements: Add products with SKU, name, description, and category

FR-03.title: Purchase Order Management
requirements: Track PO status (draft, sent, received, completed)

FR-04.title: Sales Order Processing
requirements: Create sales orders from customer requests

FR-05.title: Warehouse Zone Management
requirements: Define warehouse zones and bin locations

FR-08.title: Barcode Scanning
requirements: Scan barcodes for quick product lookup
Scan for receiving, picking, and counting

FR-09.title: Stock Counting and Reconciliation
requirements: Count entry by zone or full warehouse
Variance report comparing system vs physical

FR-10.title: Reporting and Analytics
requirements: Stock valuation report (FIFO, LIFO, weighted average)

FR-01.title: Plan Product Catalog Management Workflow and Contracts
FR-01.description: Review the SRS requirement bullets for product catalog management and break them into executable API, UI, data, security, and reporting subtasks. Define the workflow states, user roles, field-level validations, request/response contracts, and failure cases before implementation starts. Use these requirement drivers as the baseline: Add products with SKU, name, description, and category; Product variants (size, color, material); Barcode and QR code generation.
acceptance_criteria: The end-to-end workflow for product catalog management is documented from trigger to completion
Validation rules, role restrictions, and error scenarios for product catalog management are explicitly defined
Implementation subtasks for product catalog management can be picked up independently by engineering

FR-01.title: Implement Product Catalog Management Backend Services
FR-01.description: Build the backend services for product catalog management on React, Node.js, PostgreSQL, Redis. Create route handlers or service endpoints, model the core business operations, enforce validation and authorization rules, persist workflow state changes, and add structured error handling and audit logging. Make sure the implementation covers these behaviors: Add products with SKU, name, description, and category; Product variants (size, color, material); Barcode and QR code generation.
acceptance_criteria: Core product catalog management business rules are implemented for Inventory and Warehouse Management System
Validation, authorization, and error handling are covered for product catalog management
API/service behavior for product catalog management is testable and documented for integration

FR-01.title: Implement Product Catalog Management Frontend Flow
FR-01.description: Build the frontend flow for product catalog management with the exact forms, tables, filters, wizards, status indicators, and action buttons required by the SRS. Connect the UI to the backend contract, handle optimistic updates or refresh flows where needed, and provide user-facing validation, permissions feedback, loading states, and recovery states.
acceptance_criteria: The product catalog management user flow is accessible and responsive across supported browsers
Forms, filters, tables, and feedback states for product catalog management behave correctly
The product catalog management UI is fully integrated with backend APIs and handles loading and error states

FR-01.title: Design Product Catalog Management Persistence Model
FR-01.description: Design the persistence layer for product catalog management. Identify the core entities, relationships, indexes, status fields, history/audit tables, and reporting attributes required to support the workflow. Add migrations and seed or fixture data where that helps engineering validate the feature early.
acceptance_criteria: The persistence model for product catalog management supports required workflows and reporting needs
Indexes, constraints, and relationships for product catalog management are defined correctly
Migrations for product catalog management can be applied safely in target environments

FR-01.title: Write Product Catalog Management Automated and End-to-End Tests
FR-01.description: Write unit, integration, and end-to-end tests for product catalog management. Cover valid flows, invalid inputs, permission failures, state transitions, data integrity checks, and regression-prone edge cases pulled from the SRS. Add fixtures or mocks that make the feature verifiable in CI and local development.
acceptance_criteria: Happy path and failure cases for product catalog management are covered by automated tests
Permission checks and validation rules for product catalog management are verified
Regression coverage exists for the most critical product catalog management scenarios

FR-10.title: Design Reporting and Analytics Persistence Model
FR-10.description: Design the persistence layer for reporting and analytics. Identify the core entities, relationships, indexes, status fields, history/audit tables, and reporting attributes required to support the workflow. Add migrations and seed or fixture data where that helps engineering validate the feature early.
acceptance_criteria: The persistence model for reporting and analytics supports required workflows and reporting needs
Indexes, constraints, and relationships for reporting and analytics are defined correctly
Migrations for reporting and analytics can be applied safely in target environments

FR-10.title: Build Reporting and Analytics Reporting and KPI Logic
FR-10.description: Implement the reporting layer for reporting and analytics, including aggregate queries, KPI calculations, filters, drill-down behavior, and export-ready payloads. Ensure data shown in dashboards or reports can be traced back to transactional records and remains performant at realistic dataset sizes.
acceptance_criteria: KPI and reporting calculations for reporting and analytics return accurate results
Filters, grouping, and export-ready structures for reporting and analytics work correctly
Reporting queries for reporting and analytics perform within acceptable limits

FR-10.title: Write Reporting and Analytics Automated and End-to-End Tests
FR-10.description: Write unit, integration, and end-to-end tests for reporting and analytics. Cover valid flows, invalid inputs, permission failures, state transitions, data integrity checks, and regression-prone edge cases pulled from the SRS. Add fixtures or mocks that make the feature verifiable in CI and local development.
acceptance_criteria: Happy path and failure cases for reporting and analytics are covered by automated tests
Permission checks and validation rules for reporting and analytics are verified
Regression coverage exists for the most critical reporting and analytics scenarios
